titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Frank Chamberlin Porter Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Martha Lund Smalley

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 9, 2019 at 23:02

did.unittitle: Frank Chamberlin Porter Papers
did.abstract: Correspondence, notes, and writings document Porter's life and work. Frank Chamberlin Porter (1859-1946) was Winkley Professor of Biblical Theology at the Yale Divinity School from 1891 to 1927.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Frank Chamberlin Porter Papers (RG 119), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

chronitemset.event: Winkley Professor of Biblical Theology, Yale
Librarian of the Yale Divinity School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, writings, and collected material document Porter's life and work.

did.unittitle: Biographical Documentation
Incl. from The Beloit Alumnus
Correspondence
Chronologically arranged letters to/from Porter
Addendum: incl. letters to/from Charles C. Torrey, Benjamin Bacon; typed excerpts of letters from colleagues; typed copy of letter from H. Richard Niebuhr to Roland Bainton re. Porter,
Notes
Notes written by Porter; arranged alphabetically by topic, followed by folders of miscellaneous notes.
Miscellaneous notes
Miscellaneous notes
Miscellaneous notes
Bound volume of notes taken by H. R. Miles from lectures delivered by Porter: New Testament Theology, Old Testament Theology, The Fourth Gospel as Related to Alexandrianism
Approximately 2,000 pages of notes, which include Porter's notes on NT Theology, his "Religion of the Spirit", various jottings, notes on his colleague Benjamin W. Bacon, class outlines, etc.
Miscellaneous unorganized notes
Notes and manuscript drafts for a book, Chapters I- X
Writings
"The Apocrypha" from The Biblical World , pp. 272-279
"The Bearing of Historical Studies on the Religious Use of the Bible" from The Harvard Theological Review , pp. 253-276
Review of The Book of Jubilees in Current Literature
"Lews Orsmond Brastow" - memorial address
"Does Paul Claim to Have Known the Historical Jesus" from Journal of Biblical Literature , pp. 257-275
"The Historical and Spiritual Understanding of the Bible" from Education for Christian Service , pp. 20-48
The Ideals of Seminaries and the Needs of the Churches
"Inquiries Concerning the Divinity of Christ" from American Journal of Theology , pp. 9-29
"An Interview with New Testament Scholars" from The Biblical World
"The Jewish Literature of New Testament Times: Why Should it be Studied?" from The Old and New Testament Student , pp. pp. 71-78, 142-151
"Judaism in New Testament Times" from The Journal of Religion , pp. 30-62
Review of The Messianic Hope in the New Testament by S. Mathews from Am. J. of Theology , pp. 111-115
Review of Philo-Judaeus by J. Drummond from New Englander , pp. 127-139
"The Place of Apocalyptical Conceptions in the Thought of Paul" from J. of Bib. Lit. , pp. 183-204
"The Place of the Sacred Book in the Christian Religion"
"The Pre-existence of the Soul in the book of Wisdom and in the Rabbinical Writings" from Old Testament and Semitic Studies , pp. 205-270
"Princeton Theology"
"Prophecy and Apocalypse" from The Biblical World , pp. 36-41
"The Psalms of the Prophets" from The Biblical World , pp. 167-176
"The Problem of Things New and Old in the Beginnings of Christianity -- Primitive Christianity and Judaism: A Symposium" from J. of Bib. Lit., pp. 1-23
"The Religious and Historical Uses of the Bible"
"Religious Fellowship, Its Basis, Bounds, and Banes" - address before the New York State Conference of Religion
"The Religious Ideas of the Book of Ecclesiasticus I and II
"The Religous Ideas of the First Book of Maccabees"
"Revelation, Book of" from Hastings Dicionary of the Bible
"The Sayings of Jesus about the First and the Last" from J. of Bib. Lit. , pp. 97-110
"A Source-book of Judaism in New Testament Times -- Critical Notes" from Am. J. of Theology
"The Spirits in Prison -- a Neglected Theory Reconsidered" from New Englander and Yale Review
"The Spirit of God in the Minds of Men" - address before the N.Y. State Conference of Religion
"The Yecer Hara: a study in the Jewish Doctrine of Sin" from Biblical and Semitic Studies , pp. 93-156
"The Spirit of Christianity and the Jesus of History" (typescript)
"The Bible and the Religion of the Spirit" (typescript)
"The Place of the New Testament in the Christian Religion (typescript)
"Word and Spirit in Paul" (typescript)
"A Statement of Christian Belief"
"A System of Abbreviations"
"Things Greater and Less in the Bible" from Yale Divinity Quarterly March 1911
Printed Material
War poetry and notes
Newspaper clippings
Material related to Yale
Miscellaneous
Personal Items and Memorabilia
Including license, cards, photographs
Photographs
Family expense book
"The Anecdotage of the Grandmother of the Seminary" - writings by Mrs. Frank C. Porter
"The Porter Home in Beloit and Related Note"; letter from Porter's son to Jonathan Preus including biographical information about Porter
